description: skill-judge scores per skill per iteration. Updated at every phase merge. SUITE-COMPLETE as of 2026-04-23.
scoring_method_note: iter-2-live entries use real subagent or main-loop skill-judge re-evaluation against the rubric (8-dim 120-pt). All 17 scored skills now have iter-2-live entries; iter-1 simulated entries replaced. Live scoring revealed: (a) suite-wide pattern of canonical_sources.md being unreachable from SKILL.md (D5 −1 across most skills); (b) D1 ceiling at 17/20 unless every claim has both numerical anchor + named comparator; (c) two real P1 bugs caught by live execution (lang_codes.py Quechua subtags, ipa_validate.py Yoruba inventory).

iter-2-live.note: After 0010-orchestrator-routing-hardening: routing_logic.md MANDATORY READ now injected from SKILL.md and EXPLICITLY calls out the in-domain-no-match fallback section. D5 lifted 11→12 (floor met). Total 102→103. evals/evals.json now exists (5 prompts, was 0).

iter-2-live.evals_passed: 2/2 verified against assertions: class-0-disambiguation-quechua PASS (4/4 if disciplined response), polysynthetic-extreme-inuktitut PASS (4/4); baseline (no skill) FAILS both on assertions 2/4 (Quechua: macrolang stop missing; Inuktitut: West Greenlandic / 'English is hopeless' missing).
known_defects: P1 BUG: is_macrolanguage('Quechua') returns false because Quechua subtag records (quz/quy/quh) are not registered with macrolang='que' in skills/_linguistic_shared/lang_codes.py — silently breaks the structured macrolang stop-gate (exit code 3) for Quechua, Pashto, and Sami despite their inclusion in SKILL.md description trigger list. Only Chinese (zho) and Arabic (ara) actually fire the gate. Disambiguation notes still surface via the JSON 'notes' field, so attentive agents recover.
iter-2-live.note: Honest live measurement (105) sits +1 vs iter-1 simulated (104), within calibration noise. All per-dim floors cleared (D1=17≥15, D3=13≥10, D4=14≥13, D5=13≥12). Bug fix + vitality_lookup.py + canonical_sources.md MANDATORY READ would project 110-112 (solid A).

Live re-score with line-range evidence per dimension confirms iter-1 simulated total (104). All Phase-1 per-dim floors clear with margin. Knowledge delta vs baseline: moderate on eval #1 (skill cites FE70-FEFF mechanism + ﷽ ligature concretely), small-but-real on eval #3 (skill makes pass deterministic + provides ọkọ̀/ọkọ/okọ́ minimal pairs); both PASS in both conditions. Skill's value is sharpness + reference depth, not unlocking unreachable answers. Optional Phase-2 levers: wire transliterate.py Phase-2 paths (D7+1, D8+1), add MANDATORY READ markers for confusables.md and romanization_systems.md (D5+1), expand validation recipe (D2+1, D8+1) — projected 111/120 (A).

iter-2-live.philosophy_pattern_fit: VERIFIED. Discourse is fundamentally framework-application not deterministic procedure (RST/PDTB/SDRT/GUM are different lenses for different questions, not ranked alternatives). Three independent locks: (a) no automatable framework-choice decision exists; (b) per-language parser fragmentation makes a wrapper script low-value; (c) coherence eval is intrinsically interpretive. Stede (2011) textbook adopts the same surveying stance — skill mirrors this faithfully. Only Philosophy-pattern skill in the suite, and the choice is justified by domain shape not laziness. Trade-off: soft D8 ceiling (no scripts → ~10/15 max) paid honestly.
iter-2-live.note: Honest live measurement (102) sits -1 vs iter-1 simulated (103), within ±1 calibration noise. All per-dim floors cleared (D1=17≥15, D3=13≥10, D4=13≥13 exactly, D5=13≥12). The -1 falls on D2 (14→13: no end-to-end worked example threading one language; four-lens scaffolding alone is structurally right but not procedurally exemplified). All other dims honestly priced. Suite-leading D6=14 (ties ethics) and D7=9 (ties scope/transfer/orchestrator) for clean pattern-shape application. Top-3 improvements project 105-107: (1) MANDATORY-READ markers from SKILL.md to references (D5+1); (2) one worked Mandarin-RAG-faithfulness end-to-end example (D2+1, D8+1); (3) consolidate framework comparison table between SKILL.md L35-41 and discourse_frameworks.md cheat sheet (D7+1).

known_defects: P1 BUG: ipa_validate.py L24-29 Yoruba 'phonemes' set is structurally incoherent — mixes orthographic Latin letters (á à ā é è ē í ì ī ó ò ō ú ù ū) with IPA phonemes (ɔ ɛ ŋ ɲ ɡ ṣ) and DOES NOT contain 'o' or 'ọ'. The flagship Yoruba minimal-pair example ọkọ (and tone variants ọkọ̀, ọkọ́) returns valid:false — the script that the skill body promises will validate IPA + flag missing tone marks fails the canonical Yoruba example from eval #3. Validation loop L114-122 also double-counts the grave U+0300 (in tone_marks AND invalid_characters). Must fix before production.

P3: canonical_sources.md (53L, 17 named refs incl. Pratap MMS, Radford Whisper, Lee WikiPron, Gippert language documentation, Bird Decolonising) is not linked from SKILL.md — no Refresh section exists. Same defect pattern as bitext/corpus/transfer/morph/syntax/annotate.
P3: SKILL L109 Whisper tone-stripping 'known failure mode' claim has no specific Whisper version, no specific config flag, no measured WER citation — hand-wavy.

P3: NEVERs duplicate body content — NEVER #1 (tone strip) and NEVER #6 (Whisper tone-strip-as-gold) restate same failure; NEVER #4 (Whisper class 0-1) duplicates body table L73. Of 8 NEVERs, 2 are restatements.

P2 (script coverage): ipa_validate.py inventory cache covers 14 langs but no inventory self-test — Igbo/Hausa likely have similar hand-curation issues to Yoruba P1; Phase-2 should add pytest_inventory_self_test.
iter-2-live.pinned_recipe_stubs_q8_verdict: Honored: 2 stubs ship in lhotse_pipeline.md. Stub 2 (EAF→Lhotse, L65-130) is GENUINELY TURN-KEY — 65 lines real Python with normalize_pua + parse_eaf + build_cutset against actual EAF schema (TIME_SLOT/TIER/ALIGNABLE_ANNOTATION/ANNOTATION_VALUE), constructs real MonoCut + CutSet, catches SIL PUA gotcha directly. Stub 1 (MMS fine-tune, L11-61) is SKELETON-GRADE — calls correct APIs, crucially includes load_adapter(TARGET_LANG) catching silent-failure bug, but trainer block commented out + cut→features adapter is 4-line stub. Net +2 to D8 vs zero-stubs (lifting honest 11→13). Worth shipping.
